title: kbsc reload serves HTML for module script (MIME type text/html, favicon 500)
description:
### Summary
Reloading kbsc web console returns module scripts with MIME type text/html and favicon 500s, breaking page load.

### Environment
- Command: kbsc (embedded console server)
- Port: 4242
- Action: browser reload after kbsc start
- Also trying ./dev.sh to compare (pending)

### Observed
- Browser console:
  - `Failed to load module script: Expected a JavaScript-or-Wasm module script but the server responded with a MIME type of \"text/html\". Strict MIME type checking is enforced for module scripts per HTML spec.`
- Network:
  - `GET /favicon.ico` -> 500 (also logged in console: `:4242/favicon.ico:1 Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 (Internal Server Error)`).
- Page does not load.

### Expected
- Module JS assets served with correct MIME types and 200 responses.
- favicon.ico served successfully (200/304) or omitted without 500s.

### Suspected areas
- kbsc embedded assets vs filesystem fallback (asset root resolution may hand back index.html for JS requests).
- MIME detection for module scripts (.js/.mjs) and favicon handling in asset server.
- Missing embedded assets bundle or copy step.

### Next steps
- Reproduce with kbsc and with ./dev.sh (apps/console dev server) to isolate server vs build assets.
- Inspect asset serving path (serve_asset/serve_asset_from_filesystem) for MIME and error handling.
- Verify embedded assets present for kbsc build; ensure .js/.mjs served with correct Content-Type and favicon resolved.
creator: unknown

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Investigation + fix in progress:
- Reproduced kbsc favicon 500 and module MIME issue; root cause was fallback handler using Path extractor with no params, producing 500 "Wrong number of path arguments" and returning text/plain/html for unmatched assets (incl. favicon).
- Added explicit routes for /index.html and /favicon.ico (favicon now 204 instead of 500) and changed fallback to use OriginalUri and serve assets correctly.
- Tested locally with kbsc on port 4242: / returns 200 html, /assets/*.js return 200 with text/javascript, /favicon.ico returns 204 (no asset), no MIME mismatch errors.
- Changes: rust/src/bin/console_local.rs (fallback, favicon route, index route); ran cargo test --locked --all-features --lib --bins --tests (green).
- Next: rebuild kbsc and retry browser reload; if ./dev.sh still shows "unable to reach server", likely API connectivity in dev setup (dev server is Vite + API on separate ports), we can dig into that after verifying kbsc fix.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Closed: fixed kbsc asset serving (module MIME + favicon) and ./dev.sh API errors. Changes: console_local.rs fallback/index/favicon routes; npm install in apps/console (express-rate-limit present); rebuilt kbsc via cargo build --locked --bin kbsc. Verified kbsc reload and dev mode both work.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Reopened. Confirmed root cause pattern: multiple stale kbsc processes, including old behavior still listening on :4242 while newer builds bind alternate ports. This causes mixed results (favicon 500/MIME errors) depending on which process serves requests. Implementing deterministic port-conflict handling and dev.sh startup guardrails now.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Progress update:
- Implemented deterministic port-conflict handling in kbsc: prompt-based fallback only in interactive TTY sessions; non-interactive runs now fail fast with explicit CONSOLE_PORT guidance (no silent fallback).
- Added startup diagnostics for repo/data root and requested vs bound port.
- Added unit tests for interactive accept, interactive decline, non-interactive exit, and overflow edge case.
- Added dev.sh guardrails: startup warnings for existing kbsc/dev.sh/listeners, explicit "USE THIS URL NOW" line, and repo+PID-scoped log files under /tmp to avoid cross-repo confusion.
- Added troubleshooting doc note for stale global kbsc installs and stale listeners.

Validation so far:
-  passes.
- Port-conflict behavior verified manually (interactive accept/decline + non-interactive exit) using test ports 64620/64630/64640.
- HTTP check on active instance: JS assets served as ;  returns 204 (not 500).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Correction to previous comment formatting: validation commands were cargo test --locked --bin kbsc (pass), interactive port-conflict checks on test ports 64620/64630, and HTTP check on a fresh kbsc instance (JS assets served as text/javascript; /favicon.ico returned 204).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Additional validation run completed before push/close:

1) kbsc unit tests
- cargo test --locked --bin kbsc
- Result: pass (29/29)

2) Full Rust suite spot-check
- cargo test --locked --all-features --lib --bins --tests
- Result: one unrelated flaky failure observed once in gossip::tests::find_free_port_returns_bindable_port (port contention).
- Re-ran failing test three times in isolation: pass x3.

3) Port-conflict runtime behavior
- Non-interactive conflict verified on port 64720: process exits with code 1 and prints explicit CONSOLE_PORT guidance (no silent fallback).
- Interactive conflict verified in TTY:
  - decline path: prompt shown, user enters n, startup aborts.
  - accept path: prompt shown, user enters y, process binds to requested+1 port and starts.

4) dev.sh startup guardrails
- ./dev.sh shows warn+continue diagnostics for existing processes/listeners.
- Explicit "USE THIS URL NOW" line printed.
- Repo+PID-scoped log file paths printed under /tmp.

5) HTTP smoke check on active kbsc instance
- / serves index with hashed JS asset path
- asset response content-type: text/javascript
- /favicon.ico response: 204 (no 500)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Closed after expanded validation and push:
- Commits pushed: d295bcc, 8546664
- kbsc tests pass (cargo test --locked --bin kbsc)
- Interactive/non-interactive port-conflict behavior verified
- dev.sh guardrail output verified (warn+continue, explicit URL, repo-scoped logs)
- HTTP smoke verified (/favicon.ico 204, JS assets served as text/javascript)
- Full all-features test suite had one unrelated transient port-contention failure in gossip find_free_port test; isolated reruns passed.
